author: y4rr
content: Investigated; closing as DEFERRED — naive par_iter over decode_view regresses native-f32 checkpoints. SD-1.5 UNet 3.4 GB on a 16-core RTX 3090 box: 315 s sequential -> 379 s parallel (poc-arxiv-rs run measurements). Root cause: decode_view for an F32 file is mmap-> owned Vec<f32> memcpy (memory-bandwidth-bound), so workers add page-fault + allocator contention instead of CPU throughput. Real win is gated on bf16/f16 checkpoints where the per-element half->f32 up-cast IS CPU-bound. Right shape: dtype-aware dispatcher (parallel for half, serial for f32). Commit 967fe376f records this in safetensors_io.rs's doc-comment so the next attempt starts from the right premise. No behavior change in this cycle.
